Tests JavaScript object key-value assignment and retrieval using assertions.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that demonstrates the behavior of JavaScript objects when using both string and numeric keys. It initializes an object `a` with several key-value pairs, where keys are a mix of numeric strings and a string with alphabetic characters. The script then uses `assert` statements to verify that accessing the object with numeric keys correctly retrieves the expected values. This illustrates how JavaScript treats numeric keys as strings when used in object properties. The code serves as a basic test to confirm the expected behavior of object property access in JavaScript.
